name: Peach & Berry Lattice Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Mixed berries (such as Strawberries, blueberries and raspberries)
Pastry for a 9-inch double crust pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F. Peel peaches if desired, then slice into 1/2-inch wedges. Cut each wedge into 3 pieces, resulting in about 3 cups. Place in a large bowl. Slice strawberries into 1/4 inch thick pieces. Halve small strawberries. Add other berries to make 2 cups. Combine berries with peaches.
2. Finely grate 1 teaspoon orange peel and place in a small bowl. Add 1/4 cup freshly squeezed orange juice. If using tapioca, steep for 20 minutes in warm water, then add sugar and nutmeg and mix well to break up tapioca. Pour over fruit and toss until well distributed. If using cornstarch, stir with orange peel, sugar, and nutmeg. Then add to fruit along with 1/4 cup orange juice. Toss well.
3. Roll out half the pastry and place in a 9-inch pie plate. Trim edges 1 inch beyond the rim of the plate. Do not prick the bottom. Roll remaining dough into a 10-inch circle, approximately 1/8 inch thick. Cut into 1/2-inch wide strips. Turn filling into prepared crust. Place half the strips over the filling about 1 inch apart. Repeat with remaining strips, placing them in opposite direction to form a diamond pattern. Trim strips so they are even with the edge of the pie. Bring the overhanging bottom crust up and over the lattice strips. Gently press together to seal edges and flute.
4. Beat egg with 2 teaspoons water in a small bowl. Brush pastry with egg mixture. Bake on the bottom rack of the preheated oven at 425°F for 10 minutes. Then reduce heat to 350°F and continue baking for 40-45 minutes or until filling is bubbly and crust is golden.
Per serving: 225 calories, 2.9 g protein, 10.5 g fat, 30.8 g carbohydrates, 0.9 mg iron, 13 calcium.
